messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "ce-hyperscale-flow-mirage",
 "title": "Year-1 hyperscale demand lands in Ireland, Sweden and Belgium — the 0.44 CE slice is a global split painted on the wrong map",
 "narrative": "seg.hyperscale rescales Synergy's global 44–48% capacity share onto Central Europe via a flat 0.91 carve-out. But ~60% of Europe's operational hyperscale self-build capacity sits in Ireland, the Netherlands, Sweden and Belgium — three of the four outside CE-7 entirely, and the fourth banned from new hyperscale construction. The 2026 self-build wave (4.2 GW, +24% YoY) will follow existing land banks and secured grid positions, i.e. mostly outside the venture's geographic scope, while inside CE-7 hyperscalers consume capacity as colo pre-lets procured through global frameworks. The model's largest segment is substantially a demand flow the venture cannot geographically reach in Year 1, and the 'sell to large operators first' strategy is aimed at it.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "seg.hyperscale"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "European hyperscale self-build additions of 4.2 GW in 2026 (+24% YoY) concentrate where the operational base already sits: ~60% in Ireland, NL, Sweden, Belgium as of Q4 2025",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c12",
    "digest:seg-mix#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Within CE-7 the two plausible hyperscale destinations are gated — NL closed to new hyperscale builds outside two municipalities, Frankfurt queued 24–36 months — so CE's realized hyperscale share of the Year-1 demand flow runs well below the flat 0.91 rescale of the global split",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-NL#c7",
    "digest:geo-DE#c15",
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "seg.hyperscale at 0.44 overstates the in-scope segment; the overstatement propagates into the large-operator buyer cell the venture leads with, compounding the channel-mix uncertainty already flagged there",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The node is marked triangulated across multiple Synergy vintages, and the trajectory (41→48%) is genuinely well-evidenced — but every vintage is global. The flat 0.91 carve-out looks like a conservative haircut, so the review instinct is satisfied; nobody asks whether the geography of hyperscale capacity is itself skewed away from CE-7, because the ledger has no node where that question lives.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "A Europe-scoped capacity split (CBRE, Synergy custom cut, or DC Byte) showing CE-7 hyperscale-operated share at or above ~0.40, or two-plus announced 2026 CE-7 hyperscale self-build campuses breaking ground outside the NL exception zones.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The 60% concentration outside CE-7 is a hard Q4-2025 datapoint and the CE gating mechanisms are enacted; uncertainty remains because hyperscalers also occupy CE colo capacity (leased, not self-build), which partially preserves the segment's PDU demand inside scope.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.32,
   "note": "CE-realized hyperscale slice shrinks in absolute terms; not a mix shift to siblings — the demand flows to geographies outside scope"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "CE-7 share of new European hyperscale self-build MW",
   "where": "CBRE European data centre quarterly updates; DCNN/DC Byte self-build geography breakdowns",
   "threshold": "CE-7 receiving <15% of new self-build MW over two quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Hyperscaler campus announcements and permitting filings inside CE-7",
   "where": "Google/Microsoft/AWS EU infrastructure press; German and Polish municipal permitting dockets",
   "threshold": "Zero new CE-7 self-build campus starts over two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a CE-7-scoped segment split (hyperscale/colo/enterprise by MW) as a custom cut from Synergy or DC Byte",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "note": "Directly replaces the global 0.91 rescale with regional data"
  },
  {
   "action": "Rebalance the Year-1 pipeline toward colocation and enterprise cells that are physically located in CE-7, deferring the hyperscale motion until regional presence is proven",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "demand-discontinuity",
 "riskId": "risk.demand-discontinuity.ce-hyperscale-flow-mirage"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

German and Polish municipal permitting dockets","threshold":"Zero new CE-7 self-build campus starts over two consecutive quarters","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission a CE-7-scoped segment split (hyperscale/colo/enterprise by MW) as a custom cut from Synergy or DC Byte","type":"information","voiNodeId":"seg.hyperscale","note":"Directly replaces the global 0.91 rescale with regional data"},{"action":"Rebalance the Year-1 pipeline toward colocation and enterprise cells that are physically located in CE-7, deferring the hyperscale motion until regional presence is proven","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
